title: For Lucid Motors Stock, Robotaxis Are Now the Whole Story
text: Lucid Group has experienced significant declines, dropping 72% over the past 52 weeks and falling another 40% year-to-date. The company's valuation now heavily depends on its autonomous vehicle ambitions rather than traditional EV sales. In Q1 2026, the company reported disappointing earnings with revenue of $282.5 million, up 20% year-over-year but missing analyst expectations of $377 million. Loss per share was $2.82, worse than expected -$2.30. Adjusted EBITDA loss was $780.6 million. Free cash flow declined to -$1.44 billion. Uber has agreed to buy at least 35,000 Lucid vehicles for a future global robotaxi network with an additional $200 million investment. The company aims to launch commercial robotaxi services in California's Bay Area later in 2026, utilizing the Lucid Gravity SUV platform. Consensus analyst rating stands at hold with an average price target of $11.29, suggesting approximately 78% upside potential. However, analysts suggest meaningful gains require tangible, scaled cash flows from robotaxi operations rather than speculation alone.
